zotero.abstractNote: We study the approximation properties of randomized signatures as reservoir systems for learning rough dynamics. Randomized signatures are a computationally efficient alternative to the classical signature transform, which is a powerful tool for feature extraction from time series. We prove that randomized signatures are universal approximators for continuous functions on compact sets of paths, and we provide a quantitative bound on the approximation error. We further demonstrate the effectiveness of the proposed approach on several synthetic and real-world datasets, showing that randomized signatures can achieve competitive performance with significantly lower computational cost than classical signatures.
